rationale.R1.2: Verifying evidence support requires checking that conclusions follow logically from the reported numbers. The artifact contains a critical logical inversion: the Cramér–von Mises statistic W² = 0.084 (FlowPhot) > W² = 0.031 (baseline), yet the paper asserts this larger value confirms FlowPhot is closer to the ideal uniform PIT distribution — the opposite of what a lower-is-better divergence statistic entails. Detecting such inference-chain failures is load-sharing with R1.3 for this delegation.
rationale.R1.3: The delegation is precisely 'verify the evidence supports the claims,' making evidence-claim alignment the primary load-bearing responsibility. Every quantitative assertion must be audited: η_0.15 (3.1% vs 4.7%), σ_NMAD (0.021 vs 0.027, and 0.029 vs 0.034 in faint bin), the ~30% improvement figure (σ_NMAD yields ~22% and η_0.15 yields ~34%, so 30% is an approximation that warrants disclosure), and the PIT W² values whose directional interpretation is inverted in the text.
